page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, utilizing dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing 1.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU test registers a strong score of 2684.89, placing it in the top 25% of tested instances. However, multi-core stress-ng performance drops to a weak rating of 2695.68, and the Geekbench multi-core score of 1813.0 is categorized as poor. Memory performance is also constrained, with a PassMark memory score of 1007.44 and read-only large block memory bandwidth at 29.49 GB/sec, both landing in the bottom decile or quartile. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression maintain average performance.

The primary tradeoff of the n2d-highcpu-2 is its highly constrained memory capacity of 1.0 GB per vCPU, which limits its utility for memory-intensive applications. This resource density makes it a cost-efficient option for lightweight, compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints or local storage. It is suited for basic single-threaded processing, simple text processing, and lightweight utility scripting. It is poorly suited for database operations, software compilation, or web serving, as evidenced by its low PassMark database score of 906.95 and static web serving throughput of 2.41 GB/sec.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor and 2.0 GB of system memory. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance allocates a low 1.0 GB of memory per core. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng scoring 2684.89, but multi-core and memory-bound operations perform poorly, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 1813.0 and a PassMark memory score of 1007.44. The server offers a cost-efficient profile for lightweight, compute-bound workloads with low memory requirements. It is suitable for single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, and simple utility scripts, but is constrained for database operations, software compilation, and web serving, where memory bandwidth and multi-core throughput are critical.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 server with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 2 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but is constrained by low memory and multi-core limits.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 server featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 2 GB RAM, and strong single-core CPU performance.
tagline: An AMD EPYC powered dual vCPU instance offering strong single core capabilities for lightweight tasks with low memory requirements.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 2.0 GB of RAM and 32 MB of L3 cache
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic text processing
Constrained for database operations and multi-core workloads
